futureIntegrationCompatibility: Patient external identity uses a normalized clinic/source-instance mapping to avoid vendor coupling.
Future connectors may resolve patients and reference durable patient/mapping IDs.
Future note delivery, retry, failure and audit evidence can reference durable identities without changing the core patient uniqueness model.
No connector, note synchronization, outbox or vendor API design is included.

approvalGroups.title: Tenant identity and clinic membership model
approvalGroups.ownerResolution: Use separate global platform-operator authorization and clinic memberships. super_admin is global and restricted to the platform owner/authorized technicians. admin, clinical_staff, doctor and auditor are clinic-scoped. A user may have multiple clinics and different roles per clinic. Authorization keys use auth.users.id, never email alone.
approvedRefinements: Add application-owned platform_operator_roles (or equivalently named global operator authority) keyed to auth.users.id; do not represent global scope with a nullable clinic membership.
Add clinic_memberships keyed to auth.users.id and clinics.id with role, lifecycle status, invitation/activation/suspension/revocation timestamps and audit actors.
Controlled one-time global-super-admin bootstrap.
Global super_admin creates clinics; clinic creation and initial admin membership are atomic or recoverably idempotent.
Clinic admins manage allowed memberships only in their active clinics and cannot grant super_admin.
approvalGroups.productionBehaviorImpact: Replaces Production email-keyed global user_roles and client-side role authority with durable global-operator and multi-clinic membership authorities.
applicationPrerequisites: Authorization-context resolver returning global operator status plus active clinic memberships.
Server membership administration, invitation acceptance, suspension/reactivation/revocation and break-glass services.
Update AuthGuard, AppShell, settings, assistant and investigation consumers.
Idempotent clinic/bootstrap orchestration and owner-only bootstrap command/process.
dataPrerequisites: Inventory user_roles and auth.users email matches, duplicates, orphans, inactive users and unknown roles.
Create an owner-approved mapping of every current role to global operator or clinic membership; do not infer clinic scope.
securityPrerequisites: Protect first/last global super_admin and prohibit clinic admins from global grants.
migrationPrerequisites: Forward schema and data migration from user_roles after mapping approval.
Coordinate policy replacement and application rollout.
validationRequirements: Multiple clinics and different roles per clinic.
Global super_admin access and audit.
Clinic-admin delegation boundaries.
Bootstrap idempotency, last-admin protection, suspension and revocation.

approvalGroups.title: Anonymous and PUBLIC application access removal
approvalGroups.ownerResolution: Remove anonymous direct access to every SteriSphere application table. Browsers require authenticated sessions and authorization. Future public workflows use narrow server/API boundaries only.
approvedRefinements: Shared workstation/scanner/kiosk workflows are not exempt from authentication; their specific authentication mechanism must be designed before release.
Service-role use requires explicit caller authorization.
approvalGroups.productionBehaviorImpact: Tightens Production by replacing broad PUBLIC policies and may stop anon-key browser queries that currently succeed.
applicationPrerequisites: Inventory and replace any browser flow that depends on an unauthenticated session.
Define authenticated shared-workstation and scanner behavior.
securityPrerequisites: Anonymous denial regression suite for clinical and deployment data.
Review every NEXT_PUBLIC anon-client query under authenticated and membership-scoped policies.
migrationPrerequisites: Deploy compatible application authentication before or atomically with Production policy removal.
validationRequirements: Anonymous SELECT/INSERT/UPDATE/DELETE denial on all application tables.
Authenticated member success, non-member/cross-clinic denial, login continuity.

approvalGroups.title: Role authorization and privilege administration
approvalGroups.ownerResolution: All role and membership changes are audited server operations. Global super_admin manages platform roles and memberships; clinic admin manages only permitted clinic memberships and cannot grant super_admin.
approvedRefinements: Invitation acceptance, role assignment/removal, suspension/reactivation and revocation emit audit evidence.
Document a break-glass process for loss of all global administrators.
approvalGroups.productionBehaviorImpact: Eliminates Production PUBLIC role administration and replaces direct user_roles browser access.
applicationPrerequisites: Server authorization and membership-management services.
Replace settings direct role queries/mutations with authorized APIs.
UI for invitation and lifecycle state with bounded delegation.
dataPrerequisites: Map current user_roles records before transition.
securityPrerequisites: Authorization checks for every membership operation.
migrationPrerequisites: Migrate role policies and records only after server/UI readiness.
validationRequirements: Global and clinic delegation matrix.
Unauthorized grant, self-escalation, cross-clinic and last-admin negative tests.

approvalGroups.title: Patient and traceability access
approvalGroups.ownerResolution: Each ordinary patient belongs to exactly one clinic. Each trace uses the same clinic as its patient and pack/cycle evidence. Access is clinic-scoped; global support access is super_admin-only and audited.
approvedRefinements: Do not guess ambiguous historical ownership. Classify rows as resolved, quarantined, or explicitly global only where valid.
Strict tenant constraints wait for preflight resolution.
approvalGroups.productionBehaviorImpact: Adds tenant ownership and replaces Production PUBLIC patient/trace access.
applicationPrerequisites: Pass trusted clinic context through patient import/create and trace workflows.
Update patient, history, trace, pack-detail and report queries for authorized clinic context.
Provide reviewed quarantine/resolution operations.
dataPrerequisites: Read-only ownership preflight joining patients, traces, packs and cycles.
Report missing, conflicting, cross-clinic, orphan and duplicate ownership.
Quarantine manifest with row identity, evidence, reason and resolution status; no automatic backfill.
securityPrerequisites: Clinic membership policies and audited super_admin support access.
migrationPrerequisites: Backfill only owner-resolved mappings, then enforce clinic keys/FKs/policies.
validationRequirements: Patient/trace same-clinic invariants.
Ambiguous row rejection/quarantine.
Role and cross-clinic access matrix.

approvalGroups.title: Cycle, load, and pack access
approvalGroups.ownerResolution: Every cycle belongs to one clinic; load items and packs derive clinic ownership from authoritative durable cycle/deployment context. Reads/writes are clinic-scoped and trusted server transitions follow caller authorization.
approvedRefinements: Never prefer client clinic input over durable relationships.
Historical conflicts require explicit resolution.
approvalGroups.productionBehaviorImpact: Adds cycle tenant ownership and replaces broad Production cycle/load/pack policies.
applicationPrerequisites: Update CycleWizard, cycle, pack generation/review, inventory, dashboard and investigation workflows.
Move privileged state transitions to authorized server workflows where required.
dataPrerequisites: Read-only preflight for cycles, loads and packs with missing/orphan/conflicting ownership and nullable pack-cycle relationships.
Resolution/quarantine plan; no inferred backfill from untrusted fields.
securityPrerequisites: Clinic membership policies and server caller authorization.
validationRequirements: Ownership derivation, lifecycle, pack generation concurrency, role matrix and cross-clinic denial.

approvalGroups.title: Deployment, setup, provider, sterilizer, and clinic-settings authorization
approvalGroups.ownerResolution: Only global super_admin creates clinic tenants and deployments. Clinic admins manage allowed configuration inside active memberships only. Every service-role setup/deployment call requires authenticated caller authorization and durable clinic-scope verification.
approvedRefinements: Client clinic IDs are assertions, not authorization.
Preserve deployment idempotency and audit evidence.
Current persistDeploymentRunAction has no authenticated caller/membership/super_admin check before constructing its service-role client; this gate is required.
approvalGroups.productionBehaviorImpact: Tightens Production setup/settings/provider/sterilizer authorization while preserving service-role deployment mechanics.
applicationPrerequisites: Add authenticated caller resolution and global-super-admin/clinic-admin authorization before service client construction.
Separate new-clinic creation from permitted existing-clinic administration.
Propagate trusted caller/clinic authorization evidence through setup services.
dataPrerequisites: Verify clinic_id completeness and active membership for existing setup/configuration records.
securityPrerequisites: Deny unauthenticated, non-member and cross-clinic setup.
Audit actor, target clinic and deployment identity.
migrationPrerequisites: Application authorization gate first; then align Production policies before release.
validationRequirements: New-clinic global-admin only.
Existing-clinic bounded admin operations.
Direct action invocation, forged clinic ID, cross-clinic and idempotency tests.

approvalGroups.title: Runtime RPC and service-role execution
approvalGroups.ownerResolution: All 17 runtime RPCs remain server-side service-role operations with minimum ACLs, trusted search paths and durable clinic/deployment validation.
approvedRefinements: Reconfirmed: all non-test .rpc calls are in deployment Supabase repositories; no browser-side RPC call exists.
Caller-supplied p_clinic_id is never sufficient authorization.
approvalGroups.productionBehaviorImpact: Preserves current server execution; tightens only ACL/search-path attributes where drift exists.
applicationPrerequisites: Complete the setup caller-authorization gate described by APPROVAL-006; no RPC call-site redesign is required.
migrationPrerequisites: Apply ACL/attribute migration only where Production differs.
validationRequirements: All 17 positive paths and execute-denial, tenant mismatch, stale lease/token, replay, malformed input and concurrency negatives.

approvalGroups.title: Trigger-helper permissions
approvalGroups.ownerResolution: Eight timestamp helpers remain trigger-only infrastructure with trusted search paths and no direct browser execution; preserve all live attachments and behavior.
approvalGroups.productionBehaviorImpact: Preserves trigger behavior and may tighten Production function ACL/search_path.
securityPrerequisites: Restrict direct execute without impairing trigger invocation.
migrationPrerequisites: Forward ACL/search-path correction only where Production differs.
validationRequirements: Trigger attachment/event/timestamp/NEW behavior and browser-role execute denial.

approvalGroups.title: Patient external-ID uniqueness
approvalGroups.ownerResolution: External patient identity is not global. Prefer a normalized patient_external_identifiers mapping owned by clinic and SteriSphere patient, with source-system type, external ID, optional source-instance/connection ID, lifecycle and integration metadata.
approvedRefinements: Uniqueness is clinic + source instance (or source system when only one instance) + external patient ID.
Core patients may have no external mapping.
Keep schema compatible with future practice-management connectors without designing connector, outbox, API client or note synchronization.
approvalGroups.productionBehaviorImpact: Replaces duplicate global Production external_id uniqueness with integration-ready clinic/source identity mappings.
applicationPrerequisites: Patient import/resolution services must use external mappings rather than treat patients.external_id as global.
dataPrerequisites: Inventory duplicates, nulls, source systems, clinic ownership and possible multiple source instances.
Do not enforce until ambiguous identity/ownership is resolved.
securityPrerequisites: External mappings inherit patient clinic access and protect connector metadata.
migrationPrerequisites: Introduce mapping, migrate verified identities, then retire/relax legacy global constraints.
validationRequirements: Same external ID across clinics/instances.
Same-instance collision.
Null/no mapping and lifecycle transitions.
Future connector can resolve patient identity and reference delivery/audit records by durable IDs.

approvalGroups.title: Provider and sterilizer uniqueness
approvalGroups.ownerResolution: Provider and sterilizer names are clinic-scoped, never global. Deployment keys are immutable and clinic-scoped. Historical relationships use durable IDs. Archived records remain addressable and their normalized names remain reserved within the clinic.
approvedRefinements: Safest archived-name rule: no reuse while an archived record exists. This avoids ambiguous support/history and accidental reactivation collisions; a future explicit merge/rename process may release a name.
approvalGroups.productionBehaviorImpact: Replaces Production global name uniqueness with clinic-scoped all-lifecycle name reservation.
applicationPrerequisites: Update setup/settings duplicate validation and error handling to include clinic and lifecycle.
dataPrerequisites: Inventory normalized collisions per clinic and lifecycle before migration.
migrationPrerequisites: Create clinic-scoped normalized indexes only after collision resolution; preserve immutable keys and IDs.
validationRequirements: Cross-clinic reuse, same-clinic rejection including archived rows, reactivation, immutable keys and concurrency.

approvalGroups.title: Workstation ordering
approvalGroups.ownerResolution: Use the Production-compatible non-unique (display_order, name) workstation index. It provides deterministic ordering but no uniqueness rule.
approvalGroups.productionBehaviorImpact: No intended Production or application behavior change.
migrationPrerequisites: No migration unless normalized definition comparison finds drift.
validationRequirements: Index definition, plan use, ties and null display order.

approvalGroups.title: Hardware, printer, agent, and provisioning defaults
approvalGroups.ownerResolution: Database owns durable valid hardware state; deployment owns idempotent planned defaults/templates/associations; application presents and validates product choices; environment configuration owns endpoints, protocols, ports, secrets and agent locations.
approvedRefinements: Planned/inactive rows may be incomplete; active rows must satisfy operational constraints.
Loopback/private HTTP is allowed only when an explicit local deployment mode is enabled.
Cloud/public mode never silently defaults to localhost or arbitrary HTTP.
Local-mode boundaries and validation rules are activation prerequisites, not unresolved baseline architecture.
approvalGroups.productionBehaviorImpact: Removes environment-specific Production defaults and validates lifecycle-dependent hardware state.
applicationPrerequisites: Define local-mode configuration UX and activation validation.
Stop relying on database localhost/Wi-Fi/port defaults as product truth.
dataPrerequisites: Inventory NOT VALID printer checks, localhost/private/HTTP URLs, connection settings and active incomplete hardware.
securityPrerequisites: Validate scheme/host by mode; keep secrets out of durable public configuration; authorize device provisioning by clinic.
migrationPrerequisites: Application/environment support first, then forward default/check cleanup.
validationRequirements: Local/private/cloud URL matrix, USB/network printer, planned/active/archived transitions and idempotent provisioning.

approvalGroups.title: Platform and Supabase prerequisites
approvalGroups.ownerResolution: Supabase owns auth, storage, managed extensions and platform roles. The application baseline asserts prerequisites, references required objects and never recreates platform internals. Storage is excluded absent runtime evidence.
approvalGroups.productionBehaviorImpact: Preserves Production platform ownership and adds fail-fast baseline assertions.
securityPrerequisites: Verify required roles and auth helpers without granting platform ownership.
migrationPrerequisites: No application Production migration unless prerequisite drift is discovered.
validationRequirements: Compatible/incompatible prerequisite checks and proof no platform objects are created.

approvalGroups.title: Function, index, constraint, trigger, and default normalization
approvalGroups.ownerResolution: Use normalized Production-compatible evolved definitions; ignore identifier truncation only when semantics match; treat genuine differences as explicit migrations; exclude executable superseded/history while preserving provenance.
approvalGroups.productionBehaviorImpact: Preserves deployed semantics except separately approved changes and prevents superseded objects entering the baseline.
migrationPrerequisites: Forward migration for genuine semantic differences only.
validationRequirements: Normalized definitions/hashes, dependencies, signatures, constraints, indexes, triggers, defaults and counts.

approvalGroups.title: Audit access and tenant ownership
approvalGroups.ownerResolution: Clinic audit events have one clinic; explicit global/system events may omit clinic only with a scope classification. Trusted server/database paths write audits. Clinic admin/auditor read permitted clinic events; global super_admin has audited platform-wide support access.
approvedRefinements: Nullable clinic_id never implies global scope.
Preserve actor, clinic/scope, action, target, timestamp and relevant metadata.
Service-role bypass does not excuse tenant attribution.
approvalGroups.productionBehaviorImpact: Adds audit scope/tenant attribution and replaces broad authenticated Production insert/read.
applicationPrerequisites: Central trusted audit-write service/context.
Update audit page, reports, dashboard and assistant activity for scoped reads.
dataPrerequisites: Classify historical rows as clinic, explicit global/system, or quarantined unknown; do not guess.
securityPrerequisites: Append-only controls, role-scoped reads and audited global support access.
migrationPrerequisites: Resolve/quarantine history, add scope/clinic invariants, then replace policies.
validationRequirements: Tenant/global/unknown classification, append-only writes, role matrix, cross-clinic denial and service attribution.
